contaminant.name: Tree Sap and Resin
contaminant.description: Tree sap forms as a sticky organic exudate when trees release it onto surfaces during injury or environmental stress, creating a tacky layer that hardens unevenly over time. This contaminant stands out from similar resins through its high sugar content, which leads to fermentation risks and a glossy, irregular buildup rather than uniform films. On metals like steel or aluminum, it bonds tightly and resists scraping, while on glass such as float glass, it spreads thinly but etches subtly; removal challenges include its penetration into microstructures, demanding methods that avoid substrate damage. Laser cleaning tackles this effectively by vaporizing the sap layer precisely, preserving underlying materials without chemicals or abrasion.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Multiple toxic compounds detected: Formaldehyde, Acetaldehyde, Methanol - requires enhanced protection
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: Half-face or full-face respirator with organic vapor/particulate cartridges, adequate ventilation. WARNING: Formaldehyde, Acetaldehyde - known carcinogen(s), minimize exposure

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (12 ACH) for particulate control
